note: Manually run + verified in the building session (not via skill-creator's subagent+browser-viewer loop) -- this is a personal single-user utility skill, and the automated trigger-optimization eval (run_loop.py/run_eval.py) is known-broken in local-agent-mode sessions on this machine (nested `claude -p` calls are unauthenticated here; see memory reference-skill-creator-windows). Re-run these manually after any change to SKILL.md or scripts/.

evals.prompt: make me a 64x64 pixel art knight sprite with transparent background
evals.expected_output: A QC-passing (true-grid, <=32 colors, clean binary alpha, verified crisp upscale) knight sprite PNG, produced with no manual steps beyond following SKILL.md.

evals.prompt: here's an image I got from ChatGPT, can you turn it into real pixel art [attached PNG]
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as Lane C (already-styled but off-grid), runs --method unfake (no pixeloe pass), produces a QC-passing true-grid PNG.

evals.prompt: I need a 16-bit style tileset tile for grass, tileable, game asset
evals.expected_output: Generates via Lane A or B, pixelizes, and either notes the source doesn't yet handle true seamless tiling (out of Phase 1 scope) or produces a reasonable single-tile QC-passing result -- should not silently claim seamless tiling works if it wasn't verified.

evals.prompt: make an 8-direction sprite sheet for a wizard character so I can use it in my top-down game
evals.expected_output: Generates via Lane A (not B -- Lane B fails this composition per Phase 2 findings), pixelizes the whole sheet as one unit, slices with slice_sheet.py, runs verify_facing.py. MUST honestly flag that the 8 cells will show the same pose repeated (not real rotation) rather than presenting it as a working 8-direction sheet -- this is Phase 2's confirmed local-generation limitation.

evals.prompt: give me a 2-frame walk cycle for this pixel character [attached PNG]
evals.expected_output: Uses comfy_edit.py (Qwen-Edit-2511) with two alternating-stride pose instructions against the SAME source image, pixelizes and QCs both frames, and mentions the ~22min first-cold-load cost. Should not attempt this via blind multi-panel text generation (that's the failed lever, not this one).
